title: String URI overloads should call System.Uri overloads
description: String URI overloads should call "System.Uri" overloadsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?String representations of URIs or URLs are prone to parsing and encoding errors which can lead to vulnerabilities. TheSystem.Uriclass is a safe alternative and should be preferred.This rule raises an issue when two overloads differ only by the string /Uriparameter and the string overload doesn’t call theUrioverload. It is assumed that the string parameter represents a URI because of the exact match besides that parameter type.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: String URI overload should call the System.Uri overload for proper URI parsing and validation

never_trigger_on: String overloads that already call the Uri overload
Methods without both string and Uri overloads
logic.reasoning: String representations of URIs are prone to parsing and encoding errors which can lead to vulnerabilities. The System.Uri class is a safe alternative. When a class has both string and Uri overloads of a method, the string overload should delegate to the Uri overload.

should_trigger:
public void Navigate(string uri) { client.GetAsync(uri); }
public void Navigate(Uri uri) { client.GetAsync(uri); }
should_not_trigger:
public void Navigate(string uri) { Navigate(new Uri(uri)); }
public void Navigate(Uri uri) { client.GetAsync(uri); }
